segments.s213-g145: دگرگونه بود is an adjectival predicate + copula: دگرگونه (different) precedes the past copula بود (was) for emphasis. آنچ انداختیم is a nominal/relative phrase ('that which we put/did') in which آنچ = 'that which' introduces what follows, and انداختیم is the 1st-person-plural past of انداختن used here in the sense 'we set/arranged/caused' (i.e., 'what we had done/laid out'). The whole segment reads literally 'Different was that which we had arranged.'
segments.s213-g146: بریشان is the contracted poetic sequence بر ایشان = 'against/onto them' marking the target of the action. همی is an older particle (here an emphatic/continuative element) inserted before the infinitive. تاختن is the infinitive 'to charge/attack' functioning as the object of ساختیم. ساختیم is 1st-person-plural past of ساختن used causatively 'we made/caused (to do)'. Together: 'we caused them to charge (against them).' Word order places the prepositional target before the verb and uses the infinitive as the complement of ساختیم.
segments.s213-g147: همه کوه یکسر سپاهست is a copular clause with subject then predicate: 'همه کوه' (the whole mountain) + predicate 'یکسر سپاهست' ('is entirely a host'). سپاهست is the older/poetic fused form of 'سپاه است' (is an army). The final 'و کوس' coordinates another nominal element ('and the drum'), with the copula elided — i.e. 'and the drum (is there/too)'.
segments.s213-g148: درفش از پس پشت گودرز و طوس is a nominal phrase placing 'درفش' (the standard/banner) and locating it by a stacked prepositional expression. از + پس + پشت together intensify 'from behind' — 'از پسِ پشتِ گودرز و طوس' = 'from behind the backs of Goudarz and Tūs'. Word order is noun + locative prepositional phrase; no copula is required because the location is asserted by context.
segments.s213-g149: چنان کن is an imperative ('do thus' / 'make it so'). که introduces the result/purpose clause ('so that'). چون here functions in the temporal sense 'when' (an archaic/poetic use). بردمد is an archaic/poetic verbal form meaning 'breaks forth / appears' (not one of the modern basic senses listed) and combines with the following noun phrase 'چاک روز' ('the cleft/tear of day' = dawn). The order 'بردمد چاک روز' is a poetic inversion where the verb precedes the noun phrase it governs.
segments.s213-g150: پدید آید is the verb phrase 'appear / come into view' (آید here has a jussive/subjunctive or result sense under the preceding 'چنان کن که'). از چرخ is a prepositional origin 'from the sky/heaven', and گیتی فروز is a poetic genitival/epithet construction meaning 'world-illumining' or 'bright for the world' — فروز is an adjective/adjectival epithet 'bright/illuminating' qualifying the light implied in the clause.
segments.s213-g151: تو ایدر بوی ساخته با سپاه literally reads 'you here have made a bōy with the army.' ساخته is a perfect/participial form 'have made' (completed action). بوی (literally 'scent') is used idiomatically/poetically to mean a rising report/noise/stir (a figurative 'alarm' or 'commotion') created by the army. Word order: subject (تو) + locative adverb (ایدر) + object 'بوی' with perfective 'ساخته' and instrument 'با سپاه'.
segments.s213-g152: شده روی هامون ز لشکر سیاه is a result-state clause: شده ('has become/been made') states the result; روی هامون uses 'روی' as 'on/upon' to mark location ('on the plain'); ز (poetic form of از) marks cause/agent here ('by/from'), so 'ز لشکر سیاه' = 'by the black army.' Overall: 'the plain has been made [so] on account of / by the black host' = 'the plain has become covered by the black host.'
segments.s213-g113: اگر introduces a conditional clause ('if'). دستیابی is a verbal noun meaning 'reaching/attainment' used elliptically for 'if you can reach (him)'. بشمشیر is the archaic/prepositional بِـ + شمشیر ('with a sword'), and تیز is an adjective 'sharp' modifying شمشیر. Together: 'If (you) attain/reach (him) with a sharp sword...'.
segments.s213-g114: درفش و همه نیزه are the direct objects ('the banner and all the spears'). کن is the imperative 'do/make' (here: 'tear/make into'), and ریزریز is an adverb/adjectival result meaning 'into very small pieces'—so 'make the banner and every spear into tiny bits.'
segments.s213-g115: من اینک پس‌اندر lists the speaker and temporal frame ('I, now/afterwards'). چو is a simile particle 'like/as', باد is 'wind', and دمان ('دامان') is a poetic noun 'hem/skirt'—together forming the simile 'like the wind of the hem' (i.e. a swift gust).
segments.s213-g116: بیایم is a first‑person present/subjunctive used to express imminent action ('I will come' / 'may I come'), while نسازم is a negative present/subjunctive 'I do not make/do'. درنگ و زمان are the things refused ('delay and time'). The clause as a whole means 'I will come and will make no delay nor give time' (i.e. I will not tarry).
segments.s213-g117: گزین کرد is the past compound 'chose' (گزین + کرد), with هومان as the agent (inverted word order). ز introduces the source 'from', لشکر is 'army/host', and سوار specifies 'horsemen' — 'Houman chose from the army horsemen...'.
segments.s213-g118: سپردار و شمشیرزن are the categories ('commanders and swordsmen') and سی‌هزار is the numeral placed after the nouns indicating quantity ('thirty thousand'), so the phrase reads 'thirty thousand commanders and swordsmen'.
segments.s213-g119: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when/as'. خورشید تابنده is the subject phrase 'the shining sun' (noun + participial adjective), بنمود is the past tense 'showed/appeared', and تاج is the direct object 'the crown' (i.e. 'when the shining sun displayed the crown').
segments.s213-g120: بگسترد is a past/singular verb 'he spread/extended'. کافور is the material 'camphor' as the object, and بر تخت عاج is the locative phrase 'upon the ivory throne' (بر = on/upon; تخت = throne; عاج = ivory).
segments.s213-g25: This is a coordination of two genitive noun phrases: خروش نگهبان literally 'cry of (the) guard' (noun + possessor) joined by و 'and' to آوای زنگ 'the sound/voice of the bell'. Word order is noun + possessor (poetic/genitive construction).
segments.s213-g26: تو گفتی is the 2nd-person past ('you said'). The following clause بجوش آمد is a periphrastic intransitive: بجوش (seethe/boil) + آمد ('came') => 'came seething/boiling' or 'burst forth in a surge'. از کوه سنگ is a prepositional phrase 'from the rocky mountain' (کوه + سنگ as descriptive 'rock/mountain').
segments.s213-g27: هم‌آنگه is an adverb 'at that same moment/then'. برآمد is the past verb 'arose'. ز چرخ آفتاب is a poetic prepositional phrase 'from the wheel/sky of the sun' (ز + چرخ + آفتاب); the order reflects classical/poetic word order (adverb — verb — prepositional phrase).
segments.s213-g28: جهان is the subject 'the world'. گشت is the past of گشتن used as copula 'became/turned'. برسان here functions as an archaic comparative/simile marker (≈ به‌سان / 'like/as'), so the clause says 'the world became like a sea of water.' دریای آب is the noun phrase 'sea of water' (دریای — construct form of دریا + آب).
segments.s213-g29: ز is the poetic preposition 'from' placed before the noun phrase درگاه پیران ('the court/hall of the elders'). برآمد is a past tense verb 'arose/issued forth' and here precedes the noun خروش ('cry/roar') in a poetic inversion (verb + noun) rather than the more prosaic noun‑then‑verb order.
segments.s213-g30: چنان شد که is a common result clause: شد (past 'became/was') + که introduces the result ('so that/so much that'). برخیزد is a 3rd‑person present/subjunctive form 'may/does rise' used here to state the resulting action; the verb again comes before its modifying prepositional phrase از خاک جوش ('out of the earth [a] boiling/surge'), where جوش is a noun modifying خاک ('earth in a boiling/surge state').
segments.s213-g31: بهومان is an adverbial/locative phrase 'to the hall/assembly' (here marking the addressees/location). چنین گفت is a fixed phrase 'thus he said'; کاکنون ('ka-kanun') = 'now' emphasises immediacy. بجنگ is the verbal phrase 'to fight' rendered as an exhortation/imperative ('to battle!') — the subject is omitted as is normal in commands.
segments.s213-g32: نباید is an impersonal negative modal meaning 'one must not / it must not be' followed by the verbal noun درنگ ('delay, tarrying') as its complement. همانا is emphatic ('indeed/verily') and فراوان here modifies درنگ ('much/a long delay'), so the whole phrase says 'one must not delay much' (i.e. do not delay at all). Word order places the negated modal first for emphasis.
segments.s213-g121: پدید آمد is a two-word verbal phrase (past tense) meaning “appeared/was revealed,” with the verb آمد (came) at the end as in Persian word order. از دور is a prepositional phrase placed before the verb meaning “from afar,” and گرد سپاه is a noun phrase where گرد (here = dust/cloud of dust) qualifies سپاه (“the army”), so the whole segment reads literally “the army’s dust appeared from afar.”
segments.s213-g122: This clause is SVO-ish but compact: غو (an uproar/cry) modifies دیده‌بان (the sentry), so غو دیده‌بان = “the sentry’s cry.” آمد is the past tense verb “came/rose,” and از دیده‌گاه is a source phrase “from the lookout/observation-post.” The line therefore says the sentinel’s cry came from the watch-place.
segments.s213-g123: که introduces a subordinate/explanatory clause here (“that”/“for that”), آمد is the past tense verb “came/appeared,” and ز ترکان سپاهی is a prepositional + noun phrase meaning “an army from the Turks” (ز = from, ترکان = Turks, سپاهی = army/soldiers). پدید repeats ‘appear’ as a predicate adjective/verb, so the whole phrase states that a Turkish host appeared.
segments.s213-g124: بابر is used as a proper name here and is modified by سیه (“black/dark”)—a terse NP subject—followed by برکشید (past tense, ‘lifted/raised’). گردشان is a possessive compound (گرد + ـشان) where گرد functions as “dust/cloud” and ـشان “their,” so the verb takes that object: “Babur, dark, raised their dust.” (Note: the lexical entry بابر is a name in the list and is treated as a proper noun in this line.)
segments.s213-g125: چو meaning “when” introduces a temporal clause; بشنید is the simple past “he heard.” جوشن (armour/ cuirass) is the direct object, and بپوشید is the past-tense verb ‘he put on/donned’ (the prefix بـ here is the older narrative infinitive/imperative-like prefix used in poetry). طوس is the agent/name (Tūs, the hero) placed at the end: “When he heard, Tūs put on his armor.”
segments.s213-g126: برآمد is a past-tense intransitive verb meaning ‘arose/was sounded/issued forth.’ دم بوق is an idiomatic collocation where دم (literally “breath/blast”) modifies بوق (the horn/trumpet) — “the blast of the horn.” و links the two parts, and آوای کوس = “the sound/voice of the drum” (کوس = war-drum). So the clause says the horn’s blast and the drum’s sound went up.
segments.s213-g127: This NP strings together modifiers: سواران ایران = “the horsemen of Iran” (possessive/attributive order), همه = “all/everyone,” and همگروه functions as an adverbial/qualifier meaning “together/in the same group” (poetic condensed phrasing for unity). Together they form the subject: “all the Iranian horsemen, united...”
segments.s213-g128: رده is the object meaning “rank/row/line,” and برکشیدند is the past tense plural verb ‘they raised/drew up’ (they performed the action). بر پیش کوه is a locative phrase: بر (on/upon/toward) + پیش (before/in front of) + کوه (mountain), so the clause means they raised/formed their ranks in front of the mountain.
segments.s213-g57: چنین گفت پیران که = 'Thus said Pīrān that...'. گفت is the past tense (or narrative) verb 'said' placed before the subject پیران (an instance of poetic/older word order where verb can precede subject). که is the complementizer introducing the subordinate clause. هنگام جنگ is a temporal noun phrase ('at the time of battle') with هنگام governing جنگ.
segments.s213-g58: شود سست پای شتاب از درنگ = 'becomes weak the foot of haste from delay.' شود (شد) is the impersonal/third‑singular copula/verb 'become/is become.' سست modifies پای ('weak-footed' or 'with weak footing'), and پای شتاب is a genitive/possessive idea ('the foot/footing of haste') expressed by juxtaposition in poetic diction. از درنگ gives the cause ('because of/from delay').
segments.s213-g59: سپاهی بکردار دریای آب = 'a troop/host of good conduct, a sea of water.' سپاهی is the collective noun 'host/army', and بکردار ('of good conduct') is an adjectival/epithet attached to it. دریای آب is a poetic apposition ('a sea of water') giving the image of great multitude; the genitive relation is expressed by simple juxtaposition typical of classical verse.
segments.s213-g60: شدست انجمن پیش افراسیاب = 'an assembly had formed before Afrasīyāb.' شدست is an older/poetic compound form meaning 'it became / was formed' (perfective sense). انجمن is the noun 'assembly/meeting' and پیش + proper name marks location/presence 'in the presence of / before Afrasīyāb.' Word order places the verb form first, then the noun and the prepositional phrase.
segments.s213-g61: بمانیم تا آن سپاه گران = 'Let us remain until that heavy/mighty host.' بمانیم is first‑person plural subjunctive/hortative 'let us stay / we should stay.' تا introduces the temporal subordinate clause 'until,' and آن سپاه گران is the noun phrase 'that host' with گران as a postpositive adjective meaning 'heavy/powerful' (poetic for 'mighty').
segments.s213-g62: بیایند گردان و جنگ‌آوران = 'the divisions and the warriors may come / arrive.' بیایند is third‑person plural subjunctive used here as the event the 'until' clause waits for ('until they come'). گردان و جنگ‌آوران are coordinated nouns ('divisions/troops and warriors'), listed in apposition/coordination.
segments.s213-g63: ازان پس بایران نمانیم کس = 'After that, let no one remain in the wastes.' ازان پس = 'after that / thereafter.' بایران (from بایر) is a locational/nominal form meaning 'in the barren/waste places' (poetic locative use). نمانیم is negative first‑person plural subjunctive 'let us not remain / we should not stay,' and کس follows to indicate 'no one' (in classical Persian a postverbal کس with a negative verb yields 'no one').
segments.s213-g64: چنین است رای خردمند و بس = 'Thus is the counsel of the wise, and that's enough.' چنین است is a fixed predicative formula 'thus it is.' رای خردمند is a genitive phrase 'the counsel/opinion of the wise' (رای here = counsel/plan), and و بس ('and enough') is a concluding formula meaning 'that is all/that suffices.'
segments.s213-g41: 'ز لشکر' is a poetic prepositional phrase meaning 'from the army' (ز = از). 'بشادی' is an adverb 'joyfully/with joy' modifying the coming verb. 'برآمد خروش' is a V–N sequence where برآمد (past tense 'arose/issued forth') precedes its noun خروش ('cry/roar') — a common poetic inversion where the verb appears before the noun subject/phrase.
segments.s213-g42: بفرمان پیران is an agentive prepositional phrase 'by order of the elders' (بفرمان = 'by the command of'; پیران = 'elders'). نهادند is the past tense 3rd‑person plural of نهادن 'they placed/set'; paired with گوش ('ear/attention') it forms the idiom 'نهادند گوش' = 'they lent/set their ears' i.e. 'they listened'. Word order places the governing prepositional phrase before the verb.
segments.s213-g43: سپهبد is the subject 'the spahbed' (a military title). چنین گفت is a fixed phrase 'thus said' (چنین = 'thus/so'; گفت here is the past verb 'said'). با بخردان ('with the wise/wise men') indicates the addressees — با + plural adjective-noun used as a group meaning 'to/with the wise'.
segments.s213-g44: که‌ای is introducing direct speech and contains the vocative ای ('O') with an introductory که (here functioning as 'that'/'he said'), so the line moves into an address. نامور 'renowned' and پرهنر 'full of art/skill' are attributive adjectives placed before the noun موبدان ('mobeds', Zoroastrian priests), giving the vocative 'O famed, skilled mobeds'.
segments.s213-g45: چه سازیم is an interrogative formula 'what shall we do?' — چه (what) + سازیم (1st‑person plural present/subjunctive of ساختن/ساختن used idiomatically as 'shall we do'). و این را چه دانید رای links a second question: و (and) + این را (this, with the object marker را) + چه (what) + دانید ('do you know / do you think' — 2nd person plural present) + رای ('opinion/counsel'), so 'and what counsel do you have about this?'.
segments.s213-g46: که introduces the explanatory/reason clause 'for/since'. اکنون = 'now/at present'. ز دشمن تهی means 'empty of the enemy' (ز = 'of/from'; تهی = 'devoid/empty (of)'), and ماند جای ('the place remained') uses ماند (past of ماندن 'remained') with جای ('place'), so the clause reads 'for now the place remains empty of the enemy.' Word order is typical classical/poetic Persian with complements placed before or after the verb for rhythm.
segments.s213-g47: سواران لشکر is a genitive phrase 'the horsemen of the army' (سواران = riders/horsemen, لشکر qualifying them). ز پیر و جوان uses poetic ز (= از) to mean 'both old and young' (پیر و جوان) describing the riders' range of ages.
segments.s213-g48: همه 'all/everyone' modifies the group; تیز here functions adverbially 'sharply/quickly' (a swift/keen cry). گفتند is the past plural 'they said/cried out' (used for shouting or speaking), and با پهلوان (با = 'to/with', پهلوان = 'the hero/champion') indicates the addressee of their shout.
segments.s213-g97: Noun phrase + past verb: خروش (a cry/roar) is the subject followed by آمد (past 'came/rose'). از کوه is an ablative prepositional phrase (from the mountain). The second noun phrase آوای زنگ is a possessive/genitive construction ('the sound/voice of the bell') placed after the conjunction و and linked as a second coordinated sound.
segments.s213-g98: Negated perception clause: ندید is past 'he did not see'. ایچ is an emphatic negative 'any/nothing' modifying what follows. لهاک ('haste/eagerness') is the abstract noun being qualified, and جای درنگ is a compound meaning 'place to delay/pause' (جای 'place' + درنگ 'delay'). Together: 'he saw no place for delay in that haste.'
segments.s213-g99: Adverbial phrase + verb: بنزدیک is an adverb/adverbial phrase 'near/nearby' governing the following noun phrase پیران ('the elders') as the locus of proximity; بیامد is the past verb 'came'. ز راه is an ablative adverbial 'from the road/path' giving source or direction.
segments.s213-g100: Indirect object + verbal phrase: بدو is a dative pronoun 'to him/them'. آگهی داد is a light-noun + verb collocation 'gave information/informed' (آگهی 'news/information' + داد 'gave'). ز ایران سپاه is a genitive/ablative phrase 'about/from the army of Iran' specifying the content of the report.
segments.s213-g101: Subordinate clause introduced by که 'that'. ایشان is the third-person plural pronoun 'they'. بکوه is a locative/directional phrase ('in/to the mountain'); here context requires 'in that mountain'. هماون is a demonstrative 'that very'. درند is a present/plural predicative form meaning 'are fierce/ravenous' (an archaic/poetic plural predicate).
segments.s213-g102: Resultive/continuation clause: همه 'all' is the subject, بسته functions as 'drawn up/closed (together)' implying they form a barrier, بر پیش راه is a prepositional phrase 'before/in front of the road', and گزند is the nominal object 'harm/damage' indicating their hostile intent.
segments.s213-g103: Reporting clause with verb of speech: بهومان here introduces the addressee/place (the dataset senses do not include a direct 'to him' meaning, so the exact nuance is dialectal/archaic). بفرمود is an honorific past-verb 'he/they said/commanded', and پیران is the agent 'the elders' following the verb (a typical word order in classical Persian). که introduces the reported content, and زود is an adverb 'quickly/soon' modifying the command.
segments.s213-g104: Direct reported command: عنان و رکیبت are conjoined objects 'the reins and your mount/steed' (عنان = reins/bridle; رکیبت = your mount/companion in older usage). بباید is a modal periphrasis 'it is necessary / you must' (ب- prefix + بایَست form), and بسود literally 'to/for advantage' — i.e., 'guide toward advantage' or 'turn toward the profitable/safe direction.'
segments.s213-g9: چنین گفت طوس سپهبد is a compact introductory clause: چنین = 'thus' (discourse marker), گفت is the past verb 'said' (here reported speech), and طوس سپهبد is the subject (Tous, titled 'spahbed'). The final بگیو is attached as a command to introduce the quotation that follows (see next segment).
segments.s213-g10: که‌ای پر خرد نامبردار نیو is the direct address quoted after the verb بگیو. که here introduces the content ('that'/'that is:'), and ای (embedded in که‌ای) yields a vocative sense; پر خرد is a compound adjective 'full of wisdom' (پر = 'full (of)', خرد = 'wisdom'), نامبردار = 'renowned', and نیو appears as a name/epithet (uncertain here), functioning as the person being addressed.
segments.s213-g11: سه روزست تا زین نشان تاختی is a temporal clause. سه روزست is an archaic verblike copula meaning 'it is three days' (i.e. 'it has been three days'), تا introduces the point/starting mark ('since/from this'), زین نشان = 'this saddle-mark' or 'this token on the saddle', and تاختی is the 2nd-person past 'you rode/galloped'.
segments.s213-g12: بخواب و بخوردن نپرداختی states what the person has failed to do. بخواب and بخوردن are infinitival/noun-like forms 'to sleep' and 'to eat', joined by و 'and', and نپرداختی is the negative past (you did not 'pay/attend' to them) — an idiomatic classical use meaning 'you did not sleep or eat'.
segments.s213-g13: بیا و بیاسا و چیزی بخور is a string of imperatives/advice: بیا 'come' (imperative), و 'and', بیاسا is an archaic or poetically formed command 'be at rest' / 'rest!' (here used as imperative), then و 'and', چیزی بخور 'eat something' (بخور = imperative 'eat'). The verbs are ordered as a sequence of actions the addressee should take.
segments.s213-g14: برامش و جامه بنمای سر continues the commands and gives objects/recipients: برامش (a pronominal+prepositional form) means roughly 'for him/to him' (here treated as dative), و 'and', جامه 'garment/robe', بنمای is the imperative 'show/make appear/put', and سر 'head' specifies where the garment is to be placed — 'put a robe on his head' or 'clothe him, and place a garment on his head.'
segments.s213-g15: که من بی‌گمانم که پیران بجنگ is a subordinated sentence of belief: that (که) I (من) am without doubt (بی‌گمانم) that (که) the elders (پیران) will fight. بی‌گمانم is a present-tense predicate 'I am certain', and بجنگ is a classical subordinate verb form conveying eventuality/prediction 'fight' (here rendered 'will fight').
segments.s213-g16: پس ما بیاید کنون بی‌درنگ is the consequent action: پس 'therefore', ما 'we' and بیاید is an older/formal optative/impersonal form meaning roughly 'let us come/go' or 'it is fitting that we come', followed by time adverbs کنون 'now' and بی‌درنگ 'immediately'. The sequence gives the speaker's resolution to act at once.
segments.s213-g49: که is a subordinating particle introducing the reported clause ('that...'). لشکر is the grammatical subject and گریزَان (a participial/adjectival form) with شد (past auxiliary) forms the verbal phrase meaning 'began to flee / was seen fleeing.' از پیش ما is a prepositional phrase ('from/before us') indicating the direction/position relative to the speaker.
segments.s213-g50: شکست آمد is a compact poetic clause meaning 'defeat came' (an event befell). اندر introduces the following locative/affected phrase; بداندیش here functions as a substantive/adjectival noun 'the ill‑minded / ill‑intentioned one,' and ما is possessive 'our,' so اندر بداندیشِ ما = 'upon our ill‑intentioned (one)' (poetic ordering places the event verb before the affected phrase).
segments.s213-g51: یکی is an indefinite determiner 'a certain' modifying رزمگاه (battlefield). The poetic fusion رزمگاهست = رزمگاه است ('the battlefield is'). پر precedes the nouns it fills and means 'full (of),' so پر خون و خاک = 'full of blood and dust.' The coordinating و links the two nouns.
segments.s213-g52: ازیشان (ازِ ایشان) functions here as 'for/of them' (concerning them). نه ... است is a negation with the copula: 'it is not (the) time.' هنگام بیم = 'a time of fear'; و باک adds a synonymous noun 'and dread/worry.' Overall: 'for them it is not a time for fear or dread.'
segments.s213-g53: بباید is an impersonal modal 'one must / it is necessary.' پی + noun means 'after / in pursuit of,' so پی دشمن اندر گرفت is an idiom 'to take up pursuit of the enemy.' گِرفت is the verbal stem used after بایَد to express the required action ('to seize/take' → 'to take up / pursue').
segments.s213-g54: ز مولش = 'because of / on account of his patron/master.' سزد‌گر should be read as سزد گر ('it is fitting if...') where گر introduces a conditional sense; بمانی is the present/subjunctive form 'you remain' (or 'you would remain'), and شگفت is a predicative adjective 'strange / wondrous.' Together: 'If you remain because of his patron, it would be strange.'
segments.s213-g55: گریزان is a participial form used nominally 'the fleeing (ones)'. ز باد is an ablative/simile 'as/by the wind' (portraying rapid motion). اندرآید is a poetic present 'comes in / arrives,' and بب is an interjection 'look! / behold!' placed after the clause for emphasis.
segments.s213-g56: به آید = 'he/it comes to (here/toward).' ز مولیدن uses مولیدن in the sense 'to attach oneself / to seek patronage,' so 'comes to (us) from/for seeking patronage' (i.e. coming to seek help). ایدر 'here' and شتاب ('haste') function as an exhortation—شتاب is used imperatively/elliptically to mean 'hurry!'
segments.s213-g1: سپه برنشاند و بنه برنهاد is two coordinated past actions. سپه (subject) + برنشاند (past tense verb) means he 'set/arrayed the host'; برنهاد with بنه (object) means he 'raised/established the camp' — بر- verbs here are poetic pasts meaning 'placed/caused to be'. The conjunction و links the two actions in sequence.
segments.s213-g2: وزان introduces the next clause referring back to the foregoing ('and from that / from those [events]'). کشتگان is an oblique plural 'the slain' and is the object of یاد کردن (کرد + یاد) where کرد is the light verb 'made/did' and یاد is 'remembrance/commemoration'. بسیار functions adverbially/intensifier ('much/very') modifying یاد کردن.
segments.s213-g3: ازین سان همی رفت is a frozen adverbial phrase meaning 'in this manner / thus' (از + این/ین + سان) plus همی رفت, a habitual/continuous past meaning 'kept on passing'. روز و شبان is the coordinated time phrase 'day and nights' (poetic word-order); شبان here functions as 'nights' in context (poetic usage).
segments.s213-g4: پر از غم دل is an inverted adjectival phrase: دل (heart) is described as پر از غم ('full of grief'); Persian allows the order 'پر از غم دل' for poetic effect. ناچریده لبان is a participial adjective phrase: ناچریده (untouched/unplucked) modifies لبان (lips), giving 'lips untouched/unplucked'—a terse poetic image.
segments.s213-g5: همه introduces a generalization ('all/everyone'). Two parallel nominal phrases follow: دیده پر خون ('eyes full of blood') and دل پر ز داغ ('hearts full from grief'). The repeated پر is the predicate adjective 'full (of)', and ز before داغ is the poetic ablative 'from/because of' marking cause.
segments.s213-g6: ز رنج is an ablative phrase ('from/through suffering'). روان گشته combines a noun 'روان' (spirit/soul) with the perfect participle گشته ('has become/been turned'), yielding 'their spirits have become'. The simile چون پر زاغ follows: چون = 'like/as', پر زاغ = 'a rook's feather' (پر here = 'feather'), completing the comparison.
segments.s213-g7: چو at the start = 'when'. نزدیک کوه هماون is a prepositional/locative phrase 'near the mountain Homaun' (هماون here functions as a determiner/name in context). رسید is the simple past 'he reached/arrived'. Word order is typical poetic placement of adverbials before the verb.
segments.s213-g8: بران دامن کوه is a locative phrase meaning 'upon that (mountain) skirt/foothills' (بران = 'on/thereon/at that', دامن کوه = 'the mountain's skirt/foothills'). لشکر کشید is a verbal phrase 'he drew/placed the army' (کشید, past of کشیدن, here 'to move/bring' an army). The verb governs the preceding locative complement.
segments.s213-g89: "به لهاک فرمود کاکنون مایست" = 'He said to Lahak: "Now, stop".' "به + اسم" marks the indirect object ('to Lahak'). "فرمود" is a past tense reporting verb ('he said/commanded'). "کاکنون" is an emphatic/poetic fused form meaning 'now' (کا‑ + اکنون). "مایست" is an archaic imperative/jussive form meaning 'stand/stop' (poetic equivalent of modern ایست).
segments.s213-g90: "بگردان عنان با سواری دویست" = 'Turn the reins with two hundred horsemen.' "بگردان" is an imperative ('turn!') formed from گردیدن/گردانیدن. "عنان" is the direct object ('the reins'). "با" introduces the accompaniment/instrument ('with/by'), and the numeral phrase "سواری دویست" (noun + numeral) means 'two hundred horsemen' — poetic word order places the numeral after the head noun.
segments.s213-g91: "بدو گفت مگشای بند از میان" = 'He said to him, "Do not open a gap through the middle (of the line)."' "بدو" = 'to him' (prepositional/indirect object). "گفت" is the reporting verb (past 'said'). "مگشای" is a negative imperative (م‑ prefix + گشای 'open') → 'do not open.' "بند از میان" is the object phrase meaning 'a band/row through the middle' (از میان = through/from the middle).
segments.s213-g92: "ببین تا کجایند ایرانیان" = 'Look (see) how far the Iranians are.' "ببین" is an imperative 'see/look.' "تا" introduces the extent/question 'how far/as far as.' "کجایند" is a fused interrogative + copula 'where/at what point are (they)?' and is third-person plural. "ایرانیان" is the subject 'the Iranians.'
segments.s213-g93: "همی رفت لهاک برسان باد" = 'Lahak went (with the purpose of bringing [the news]) — may he bring it.' "همی" is a habitual/continuous/aspectual particle often used in epic poetry (gives a progressive or immediate sense). "رفت" is past tense 'went.' "لهاک" is the agent (proper name). "برسان" is a jussive/intentive form 'bring/deliver' expressing purpose, and "باد" is the optative particle ('may it be' / 'may he') used poetically to express a wish or blessing.
segments.s213-g94: "ز خواب و ز خوردن نکرد ایچ یاد" = 'He gave no thought to sleeping or to eating at all.' The two prepositional phrases "ز خواب" and "ز خوردن" ('from/regarding sleep' and 'from eating') are fronted; the verb "نکرد" ('did not do/make') follows them (poetic word order). "ایچ" is an emphatic negative 'nothing/not at all.' "یاد کردن" = 'to remember/think of,' so the whole clause means he neither slept nor ate nor even thought of doing so.
segments.s213-g95: "چو نیمی ز تیره شب اندر گذشت" = 'When half of the dark night had passed.' "چو" is a temporal conjunction 'when.' "نیمی ز تیره شب" is a partitive ('half of the dark night'). "اندر گذشت" is a poetic order for 'passed' (اندر = 'in/within' placed before the verb گذشت). The clause places time-frame before the main action.
segments.s213-g96: "طلایه بدیدش بتاریک دشت" = 'He saw the vanguard on the dark plain.' "طلایه" = 'vanguard' (subject). "بدیدش" is the past verb 'saw' with the enclitic object "‑ش" = 'it/him' (he saw it/them). "بتاریک دشت" is a compressed poetic prepositional/adjectival phrase (به + تاریک دشت) meaning 'on/in the dark plain' — the preposition and adjective are fused in poetry.
segments.s213-g105: بِبر is the imperative of 'بردن' (take/capture) and here issues an order; چند functions interrogatively as 'how many' and precedes the modal باید ('must'), which expresses necessity. ز + لشکر سوار is a prepositional phrase 'from the army of horsemen' with ز introducing the source and سوار a noun 'rider/horseman' modifying لشکر ('the mounted division'). The poetic word order puts the imperative and interrogative before the modal.
segments.s213-g106: ز again marks origin/partitive 'from'; گردان is the head noun 'troop/division' and is followed by two postpositive adjectival modifiers (گردنکش 'rebellious/headstrong' and نامدار 'renowned'), a common poetic ordering where qualifiers follow the noun. Together they form the noun phrase specifying which troop is meant.
segments.s213-g107: که introduces a relative clause ('which/that') and the clause's subject is ایرانیان 'the Iranians'. با ('with') marks accompaniment/instrument ('with the banner and host'); درفش and سپاه are coordinated by و. The whole segment sets up who performed the action and with what.
segments.s213-g108: گرفتند is the simple past, 3rd person plural, agreeing with ایرانیان: 'they seized/took.' کوه هماون پناه is the object phrase: کوه 'mountain' plus هماون (here a proper/pointing name or demonstrative) and پناه 'refuge' functioning as an appositive/complement 'as a refuge'. The verb precedes its noun phrase in typical poetic style.
segments.s213-g109: ازین is the prepositional phrase 'from this' (از + این written as ین here); رزم رنج 'battle-suffering' forms the situation from which رنج 'suffering/trouble' follows. آید is the present/future-like verb 'comes/will come' and اکنون 'now' times it. بروی is a jussive/imperative-style second-person form 'go' (poetic), giving an immediate command following the announcement of danger.
segments.s213-g110: This is a sequence of imperatives. خرد (wisdom) is the object placed before the verb; تیز کن is the verb phrase 'make sharp / sharpen' with کن the imperative 'do'. چاره کار جوی is another imperative: چاره 'remedy' + کار 'the affair/matter' (genitive relation) and جوی is an archaic/poetic imperative 'seek' (from 'جستن'). The order is object — verb for emphasis, typical in epic poetry.
segments.s213-g111: گر is the conditional 'if'. آن مرد 'that man' is the subject; با governs the instrument/comitative phrase 'with the Kayanian banner' (کاویانی درفش). The whole segment sets the protasis (condition) — 'if that man, with the Kayanian standard...'.
segments.s213-g112: بیاری here functions idiomatically as 'to aid/for help' (note: this specific lexeme sense is not among the provided choices, so it is marked as not listed). شود is 3rd person singular of شدن 'become' used to express result/future: 'will become.' روی ایشان 'their faces' places the possessor after the noun (poetic order), and بنفش is the predicative adjective 'purple.' Together: 'their faces will become purple.'
segments.s213-g81: همه بی‌گمانی بچنگ آوریم is a single clause: همه (all/everyone) + adverb بی‌گمانی (undoubtedly) modifying the verb phrase بچنگ آوریم. بچنگ آوردن is a verbal compound meaning 'to take into one's grasp / to capture'; آوریم is first-person plural (classical/polite) present/subjunctive/optative used here to express intent or future action 'we shall / let us bring'. Word order places adverbial modifiers before the verb phrase.
segments.s213-g82: بد آید چو ایدر درنگ آوریم is an impersonal conditional: بد آید ('it will be bad' or 'it would be unfavorable') with چو introducing the conditional 'if/when'. ایدر 'here' is the locative, درنگ 'delay/tarry' is the noun used with آوریم; here آوریم again is first‑person plural subjunctive/optative meaning 'we should/might (cause to) tarry' — together 'if we tarry here'.
segments.s213-g83: چنین داد پاسخ بدو پهلوان: داد is a simple past 'gave' (he gave); چنین 'thus' modifies the verb. پاسخ 'answer' is the direct object; بدو 'to him' is the indirect object. پِهلَوان at the end names the speaker (the hero) in apposition. Classical Persian commonly places verb before its subject or apposes the subject after the verb.
segments.s213-g84: که بیداردل باش و روشن‌روان: که introduces reported speech or the content of the reply ('that' / 'saying that'). بیداردل and روشن‌روان are adjectival compounds (poetic epithets). باش is the imperative/present subjunctive 'be' addressing the addressee (second person). و simply links the two predicates.
segments.s213-g85: چنان کن is an imperative: 'do so' (چنان 'thus/so' + کن 'do!'). The following که introduces a reason/explanatory clause: نیک‌اختر و رای تست 'Nīkākhtar and the counsel are yours.' نیک‌اختر here functions as a proper epithet/name, رای means 'plan/counsel', and تست is the archaic enclitic meaning 'is yours' (not the unrelated modern word 'test'); the predicate 'is yours' is elliptical in classical Persian.
segments.s213-g86: که چرخ فلک زیر بالای تست is a second explanatory clause introduced by که: 'for the wheel/sky and the vault are under/above your [authority].' چرخ and فلک are paired poetic synonyms for the heavens; زیر بالای here idiomatically indicates control 'under (your) command' or 'subject to you' (زیر used in the sense 'subject to/control of'). تست again is the archaic copular/enclitic 'is yours' (sense not in the provided list).
segments.s213-g87: پس لشکر اندر گرفتند راه is a past narrative clause: پس 'then', لشکر 'the host/army' as subject, گرفتند (past, third plural) 'they took/occupied', and راه 'the road' is the object; اندر is a locative/prepositional particle ('into/on') so the phrase گرفتن راه means 'to set out on the road' or 'take the road'.
segments.s213-g88: سپهدار پیران و توران سپاه names those who set out: سپهدار (a title, 'commander') + پیران is here a proper name (Pīrān, the Sepahdar), followed by و 'and' and توران سپاه 'the Turanian host/army'. In classical Persian appositive noun groups like this commonly follow the main clause to specify actors.
segments.s213-g17: کسی را که آسوده‌تر زین گروه is an introductory noun phrase with a relative clause: کسی 'someone' takes the object/topic marker را; که introduces the relative clause 'who/that'. آسوده‌تر is the comparative 'more at ease/relieved' modifying کسی, and زین گروه functions as the standard of comparison ('than this group/company'). The whole segment means 'someone who is more comfortable than this company'.
segments.s213-g18: به بیژن بمان و تو برشو بکوه contains two coordinated imperatives/commands. به + proper name (بیژن) + بمان (imperative or jussive of ماندن) = 'remain with/at Bijan.' Then و 'and' links the second command: تو (you) + برشو (imperative 'get up/go up') + بکوه ('to the mountain'), i.e. 'and you go up to the mountain.' Word order puts the indirect/goal phrase before the verb (به بیژن بمان).
segments.s213-g19: همه خستگان را سوی که کشید is built around همه خستگان 'all the weary' with the object marker را marking them as those affected. سوی 'toward' indicates direction and که + کشید (past of کشیدن) forms a clause indicating the motion or pulling action ('whom/which he drew/toward which he drew'). The literal sense: 'he drew all the weary toward (that point/him).'
segments.s213-g20: ز آسودگان لشکری برگزید is a compact clause: ز + آسودگان = 'from the rested/at-rest ones', لشکری 'an army / troops' is the object, and برگزید is the past tense 'he chose/selected.' The order is poetic (source phrase ز آسودگان precedes لشکری), giving 'he selected troops from the rested.'
segments.s213-g21: چنین گفت begins the speech tag 'thus he said.' The following clause کین کوه سر جای ماست is a nominal clause with کین کوه 'this/that mountain' as subject, سر جای 'head/place/post' as predicate nominal, ما 'our/we' as possessor/agent, and ست (است) the copula 'is.' Word order is subject — predicate — copula (poetic). The whole conveys 'Thus he said: this mountain is our station.'
segments.s213-g22: بباید کنون خویشتن کرد راست is an obligation clause: بباید 'it is necessary / one must' + کنون 'now' sets the temporal frame; خویشتن کرد 'to do (for) oneself / to act oneself' is the verbal phrase where کرد is the past-inflectional stem used here in an infinitival sense, and راست 'right/correct' qualifies the action (i.e. 'set things right' or 'behave rightly'). The sense: 'Now we must set ourselves right' or 'we must now act properly ourselves.'
segments.s213-g23: طلایه ز کوه اندر آمد بدشت is a straightforward motion clause: طلایه 'the vanguard' is the subject, ز کوه 'from the mountain' marks the origin, اندر آمد 'came down/entered' is the verb, and بدشت 'to the plain' gives the destination. Poetic order places origin before verb and destination after it.
segments.s213-g24: بدان تا بریشان نشاید گذشت is a purpose/result phrase: بدان (ب + دان) = 'know/consider that' or 'so that,' تا introduces the consequence/purpose 'so that / until', بریشان (بر + ایشان) indicates 'against/over them' or 'toward them', نشاید 'it is not proper / should not be' expresses impossibility or prohibition, and گذشت 'passage/pass' completes the idea. Together it reads roughly 'know (this), so that it should not be possible for them to pass (over/through).'
segments.s213-g73: نمانیم is first-person-plural jussive/subjunctive negative ('let us not remain' / 'we should not stay'). تا introduces a temporal subordinate clause ('until'). نزد خسرو شوند: نزد + خسرو marks 'in the presence of the king', and شوند (3rd pl. subjunctive/optative of شدن) expresses 'they go/come/become present' — together 'until they reach the king's presence'.
segments.s213-g74: بدرگاه او is a locative phrase 'at his court/presence' (بدرگاه = court; او = his). لشکری نو is the subject phrase 'a new army/troops', followed by شوند (3rd pl. subjunctive/optative of شدن) meaning 'will be formed/come into being' — literal order is locative + subject + verb.
segments.s213-g75: ز زابلستان is a poetic prepositional phrase 'from Zabulistan'. رستم آید is simple narrative 'Rostam comes/will come' (آید = 3rd sing. present/subjunctive used narratively). بجنگ is an infinitival/adverbial phrase 'to fight' (indicating purpose: 'comes to fight').
segments.s213-g76: زیانی here is a noun 'loss/harm' and بود is the past copula 'was/will be' (narrative). سهمگین is an adjective 'grave/dreadful' modifying زیانی. زین appears in the line but does not match the listed senses (it would normally be 'from this' or similar in classical usage), and درنگ is 'delay'; overall: 'this delay was a grave loss.'
segments.s213-g77: کنون = 'now'. ساختن is the infinitive 'to make/prepare'; in Persian infinitives often follow باید ('it is necessary') and function as its complement, so ساختن باید = 'one must make/prepare'. و is the coordinating 'and', and تاختن is another infinitive 'to charge/attack', linked in the same construction.
segments.s213-g78: فسونها و نیرنگها are the direct objects (plural: 'charms/enchantments and stratagems') that go with the repeated ساختن 'to make/devise'; the word order places the objects before the verb, a common poetic pattern, so the meaning is 'to make charms and stratagems.'
segments.s213-g79: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. گودرز را uses the object/topic marker را to highlight Goudarz ('when Goudarz...'). با سپهدار طوس is a prepositional phrase 'with the commander Tūs' (سپهدار is a title followed by the name طوس). The clause is an introductory temporal clause ('when Goudarz, together with the commander Tūs...').
segments.s213-g80: درفش همایون is a noun phrase 'the auspicious/imperial standard' (درفش = banner, همایون = 'auspicious/imperial') followed by a list connected by و: پیلان 'elephants' and کوس 'war-drums'. The line lists the items brought or displayed in that moment.
segments.s213-g33: This clause supplies the topic (سواران دشمن همه) followed by the perfect verb کشته‌اند. In classical Persian the verb form -اند marks 3rd person plural perfect; here کشته‌اند reports the resultive state ‘have been killed’ (contextually passive) even though the passive would elsewhere be explicit. Word order (subject before verb) is normal.
segments.s213-g34: وگر introduces an alternative possibility (‘or if’ / ‘or else’). خسته از جنگ is an adjectival phrase meaning ‘weary from battle’ (خسته + preposition از). برگشته‌اند is perfect (3pl) ‘they have returned’ and completes the alternative clause; subject is implied the same as in the previous clause.
segments.s213-g35: بزد is a past singular verb ‘he struck/beat’. Direct object کوس (war-drum) follows the verb. The coordinate و links to the next clause. از دشت is a locative/ablative phrase ‘from the plain’, and برخاست غو is inverted word order: the verb برخاست ‘rose’ precedes the subject/phenomenon غو ‘uproar’ — a common poetic inversion meaning ‘an uproar arose.’
segments.s213-g36: همی is an archaic/poetic adverb signalling continuous or habitual action (here accompanying a past verb to give an ongoing sense). رفت is past ‘went’; پیش + سپه پیشرو functions adverbially: پیش ‘in front of/ahead of’ with سپه پیشرو ‘the army’s vanguard’ (پیشرو postmodifies سپه), so the whole phrase means ‘went ahead of the army — the vanguard.’
segments.s213-g37: رسیدند is 3rd person plural past ‘they arrived’ with the subject ترکان ‘the Turks’. The particle ب + دان here forms the archaic directional/locative بدَان = ‘to/at that’ (contracted), so بدان رزمگاه means ‘to/at that battlefield.’ This is a poetic contraction of به آن.
segments.s213-g38: همه رزمگه means ‘throughout the whole battlefield’ (همه as ‘entire/whole’ modifying رزمگه). خیمه بد بی‌سپاه is an elliptical nominal cluster: خیمه ‘tents’ + بد ‘ill/poorly’ (adverbial/adjectival) + بی‌سپاه ‘without troops/defenceless’; classical Persian often omits a copula, so the phrase describes the tents as being badly unprotected.
segments.s213-g39: بشد here is an impersonal/indefinite past ‘there came / it happened that’ (it introduces the arrival). نزد + پیران means ‘to/ before the elders’. یکی مژده‌خواه is an appositive: یکی ‘one / a certain person’ + مژده‌خواه (literally ‘seeker/holder of glad tidings’) — i.e., ‘a messenger bearing good news.’
segments.s213-g40: که introduces reported content (‘that …’). کس نیست is the negative existential ‘there is no one’ (کس used with نیست = ‘no one / none’). ایدر ‘here’ is locative; ز ایران سپاه literally ‘an army/from Iran’ — classical word order places the source/agent after the locative. The clause means ‘that there are no troops from Iran here.’
segments.s213-g137: نیایدت is a negative jussive/optative construction meaning “may it not come to you” (third-person verb with the enclitic -ت referring to ‘you’). زین here functions as the fused prepositional idea “from this” (poetic ز + این) modifying کار خود; کار خود = “this very deed / your own action” (خود is emphatic). شرم و ننگ are a coordinated direct object/patient phrase “shame and disgrace” governed by نیایدت.
segments.s213-g138: خور و خواب و آرام is a paratactic list of nouns (eating/food, sleep, and rest/quiet) describing comforts that are lacking; the clause lacks an explicit verb here so the negative/force of نیایدت from the preceding clause carries over. بر + کوه و سنگ is a simple prepositional phrase “on (the) mountain and (the) rock,” with بر = “on/upon.” Note خور is a poetic nominal form related to خوردن (‘to eat’) used as ‘food/eating’.
segments.s213-g139: چو introduces a temporal clause here = “when.” فردا gives the time “tomorrow.” برآید is the third-person singular verb “rises/appears” (classical poetic form); ز کوه = “from (the) mountain” (ز = poetic از) and آفتاب is the subject “the sun.” Word order is flexible in poetry, so the verb appears amid temporal and locational elements.
segments.s213-g140: کنم is first-person singular of کردن used causatively/constructively: “I will make / I will turn.” زین حصار تو is a prepositional + noun phrase “from this/this very fortification of yours” (زین = ‘from this’, تو marks possession = ‘your’). دریای آب is the result noun phrase “a sea of water” that functions as the complement of کنم. The structure literalizes as “I make from this your barrier a sea of water.”
segments.s213-g141: بدانی is a jussive/imperative-like verb form meaning “know” or “be aware” addressed to the addressee; it introduces a subordinate clause with که = “that.” این جای is “this place,” and بیچارگیست is a nominal predicate meaning “is misery/ pitiable condition” (بیچارگی + enclitic copula -ست). The clause reads as a direct instruction: “Know that this is a place of misery.”
segments.s213-g142: برین is the preposition بر + این (written together) meaning “on this.” کوه خارا = “flinty / hard mountain” where خارا is an adjective placed after the noun (common in older/poetic Persian). بباید is the impersonal/modal “one must / it is necessary,” and گریست is the bare verbal form “to weep / weep” that follows بباید to give obligation: “one must weep on this flinty mountain.”
segments.s213-g143: فرستاد is the simple past (third-person singular) “sent.” بپیران is the fused prepositional phrase به پیران = “to the elders” (ب- here = به). زود is an adverb “quickly/soon” modifying فرستاد. هیونی stands before the prepositional phrase as the thing/person sent; in the line it appears as a name or designation (here transcribed Hyuni).
segments.s213-g144: که introduces a causal/explanatory clause “for/because.” اندیشه ما = “our thought/intention/plan,” with اندیشه used as ‘intention’ here. دگرگونه بود is a simple nominal predicate “was different,” with بود the past copula. The clause explains the reason: “because our intention/plan was different.”
segments.s213-g65: بدو (be + 3rd-person clitic) is an archaic prepositional clitic meaning “to him/unto him” placed before the verb; گفت is the past-tense verb “said” (classical word order here: indirect object + verb + subject). هومان is the subject (proper name). که‌ای پهلوان is a vocative address (که‌ای = که ای) meaning “O champion.”
segments.s213-g66: مرنجان is the negative imperative 'do not be upset / do not take offense' (used as an exhortation). بدین is the fused prepositional phrase (به + این) = “in/at this”; the annotator split it into ب + دین, so ب = ‘to/into’ here while دین as listed does not reflect 'این' and so has no matching sense. کار is the noun 'affair/matter' and چندین روان is a nominal phrase 'several/many a soul(s)' (روان = souls/people) indicating many are engaged with the matter.
segments.s213-g67: سپاهی is the collective noun 'the army / the warriors' (head of the noun phrase). بدان is an archaic fused locative/benefactive (به + آن) meaning 'with that / by that'; دان as annotated does not match this usage. زور و آن جوش و دم is a string of coordinated nouns ('strength and that surge and breath/vigor') describing the army's force; آن repeats 'that' as a demonstrative modifying جوش.
segments.s213-g68: شدی is the past of شدن ('became' / 'was made') connecting the preceding description to the following locative/predicative phrase. روی دریا is a locative phrase 'the surface/face of the sea' (روی = surface/upon). ازیشان is aused form of از + ایشان 'because of/from them' (fused in the text). دژم is an adjective 'sullen/grim' predicated of the sea's surface; the verb + adjective structure means 'the sea's surface became sullen because of them.'
segments.s213-g69: کنون is the temporal adverb 'now' modifying the following noun list. خیمه و گاه و پرده‌سرای is a coordinated list of camp/place nouns (tent, gāh — here a kind of abode/stop or place — and پرده‌سرا, a curtained hall/pavilion). The nouns are given in a simple enumerative noun phrase typical of classical Persian.
segments.s213-g70: همه is the quantifier 'all / everyone.' مانده is the perfect/past participle used predicatively 'remain / stand' (i.e., they remain standing) and برجای is the locative adverbial 'in place.' The coordinate و introduces the contrasting clause رفته ز جای, where رفته ('have gone') plus ز جای ('from the place') states that (the people) have departed — the construction expresses the contrast between the standing tents and the departed people.
segments.s213-g71: چنان دان is an imperative/hortative phrase 'consider it so / know it so' (دان = consider/know). که introduces the content clause. رفتن here is the verbal noun 'the going / departure' used as subject, followed by ز بیچارگیست, where ز = 'from / because of' and بیچارگیست (بیچارگی + است fused) means 'is due to misery/pitiable condition.' Grammatically this is a nominal clause stating cause: 'the departure is because of misfortune.'
segments.s213-g72: نمودن is the infinitive/verbal noun 'to show' or 'the showing' used as a noun here; بما = 'to us' (dative), and پشت = 'the back / turning of the back' (object of نمودن). یکبارگیست is a fused predicate (یکبارگی + است) meaning 'is suddenness / is a one-time occurrence.' The clause says that their showing their backs to us was a sudden, single event; word order is nominal (verbal noun + indirect object + object + predicate).
segments.s213-g129: چو introduces a temporal subordinate clause ('when'). هومان is the subject (proper name). بدید is a simple past verb 'saw' placed after the subject in this poetic word order. آن سپاه گران is the direct object phrase: آن = 'that', سپاه = 'host/army', modified by the adjective گران meaning 'heavy' or 'mighty' (poetically 'formidable').
segments.s213-g130: گراییدن here is a poetic verbal form meaning 'turn(ed)/brandish(ed)' and functions as the action of the clause. گرز و تیغ سران is the object: گرز 'mace' and تیغ 'sword', with سران (genitive after the noun) = 'of the chiefs/leaders' — Persian places the possessor after the possessed.
segments.s213-g131: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so' setting the manner. گفت is the speech verb 'said' (the provided sense list does not include 'said' so mapped as -1). هومان is the speaker. بگودرز is 'to Goudarz' (ب- is the dative/allative prefix attached to the name), و طوس = 'and Tous' (طوس = the hero Tous here).
segments.s213-g132: کز introduces a subordinate content clause ('that/which' or 'that'), here giving the reported content. ایران is the place. برفتید is the 2nd-person plural past 'you (pl.) departed/left'. با introduces accompaniment 'with' and پیل و کوس 'elephants and war‑drums' are the instruments/companions of their departure.
segments.s213-g133: سوس here is the name of a place (Sūs / Susa) rather than the literal senses given, so none of the provided senses fit. شهر ترکان is a noun phrase 'the city of the Turks' (شهر = city, ترکان = the Turks). بکین is a poetic/archaic verb or cry (here functioning with the following verb); its sense in the supplied list that matches 'do/perform (poetic بکن)' is chosen, but the exact nuance is archaic/onomatopoeic.
segments.s213-g134: بدان روی is a frozen phrase meaning 'on that account / for that reason' (بدان = به + آن). لشکر is the subject 'the army/host'. برون is an adverb 'out/forth'. تاختن is the verb 'to charge' (poetic infinitive used narratively: 'charged out'). Note the poetic split of بِدان into ب + دان in the segmentation; functionally it behaves as 'for that reason'.
segments.s213-g135: کنون = 'now'. برگزیدی is the 2nd-person past 'you chose' (or 'you have chosen'). چو = 'like/as'. نخچیر means 'game/prey' and کوه 'mountain' — together a simile 'like prey of the mountain' (i.e. taken to the mountain like hunted game).
segments.s213-g136: شدستی is an archaic/poetic verbal form corresponding to 'you have become' or 'you became' (the supplied senses do not match exactly, so mapped to -1). ز = 'from' (poetic zِ). گردان = 'troop/band', توران = 'Turan' (the enemy land/people), and ستوه is an adjective 'weary' or 'sick of' used predicatively: 'you have become weary of the Turanian bands.'
